stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Will Suspend Nuclear Program as Hormuz Reopens
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump said Iran has agreed to suspend its nuclear program indefinitely, fueling optimism that a deal to end their war is in reach after Tehran announced the Strait of Hormuz was open again.
In a Friday phone interview, Trump asserted that any moratorium on Iran’s nuclear activities would not expire, saying it would be “unlimited.” Talks over a lasting agreement will “probably” be held this weekend, the president said. Spencer Far

stories.headline: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
stories.summary: Iran says the Strait of Hormuz is open — and oil tankers are racing through it. Norah Mulinda explains how markets reacted to the news and asks: Can this lead to a lasting peace agreement? (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
stories.summary: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

stories.headline: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.summary: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.headline: Iran rejects Trump claim on deal to surrender nuclear material stockpiles
stories.summary: Iran has rejected suggestions by Donald Trump that it has agreed to hand over its stockpile of enriched uranium.

stories.headline: Macron demands assurances after Iran opens Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.headline: Oil slumps as US and Iran declare Strait of Hormuz open to shipping
stories.summary: Some tankers reverse course after attempting to transit waterway amid confusion over its status

stories.headline: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.headline: Trump says Iran peace talks will ‘probably’ resume this weekend

stories.headline: Wall Street Turns Gloomy on the Dollar as Haven Demand Fades
stories.summary: Deutsche Bank AG and Wells Fargo & Co. say the dollar’s war-driven haven rally is likely over, as the US-Iran ceasefire pushes investors back into riskier assets.

stories.headline: Supreme Court hands win to Chevron, Big Oil in environmental damage case
stories.summary: The decision puts into question a $745 million judgment against Chevron to help restore coastal wetlands in Louisiana that were damaged as long ago as World War II.

stories.headline: Oil Tankers Hauling US Crude Via Panama Approach 4-Year High
stories.summary: US oil cargoes transiting the Panama Canal are close to a four-year high as Asian refiners rush to import American crude in lieu of Mideast supplies strangled by the weeks-long disruption to Strait of Hormuz shipping.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Sides With Oil Companies in Louisiana Coastal Lawsuits
stories.summary: The companies had asked the justices to clear the way to move environmental lawsuits out of state courts, to friendlier federal venues.

stories.headline: House Votes to Extend Expiring FISA Surveillance Law for 10 Days
stories.summary: The Senate approved a stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

stories.headline: Ukraine war briefing: €90bn EU loan for Ukraine to be released in second quarter
stories.summary: EU economy commissioner says Iran war is feeding Russia’s war machine; Trump condemns massive strikes on Ukraine. What we know on day 1,513The EU expects to start releasing a new €90bn loan to Ukraine in the second quarter, the bloc’s economy chief told AFP on Thursday. stories.headline: Blue Owl Co-CEOs’ Personal Loans No Longer Backed by Firm Shares
stories.summary: Blue Owl Capital Inc. Co-Chief Executive Officers Doug Ostrover and Marc Lipschultz have revised the terms of personal loans to remove the company’s shares as collateral, after turmoil in the private credit market hammered the stock’s value in recent months.

stories.headline: REAL Cookies Founders on The Future of Healthy Snaking
stories.summary: Lauren Berger and Marla Felton, REAL Cookies Co-Founders joined Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss their merger with Creations Foods and the outlook for healthy snacking. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Biotech Odyssey, Medical Device Firm Mobia File for US IPOs
stories.summary: Healthcare companies are lining up to go public with a drug developer and medical device company joining a pool of peers seeking to advance public listing plans amid robust investor demand for biotech stocks.

stories.headline: NYC’s Second-Home Tax Unites Mamdani, Hochul
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Nacha Cattan joins Katie Greifeld on "Bloomberg Real Yield."  Governor Kathy Hochul and New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani have finally found a tax that they agree on. Getting everyone else on board stands to be more complicated. Mamdani has been an eager proponent of taxing wealthier New Yorkers to close a projected $5.4 billion two-year gap in the city’s budget and to fund a slew of social programs.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Belgium Gets Cut by Moody’s in Reproof at Failure to Cut Debt
stories.summary: Belgium was lowered one step by Moody’s, underscoring how the struggle to cut one of the biggest budget deficits in Europe is besmirching the country’s status as a borrower.

stories.headline: S&P 500 Closes at Record High | Closing Bell
stories.summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
stories.summary: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”

stories.headline: US tech firms successfully lobbied EU to keep datacentre emissions secret

stories.headline: Home Office ‘red flag’ error leaves German mother separated from toddler in UK
